• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code
  • Download Rmd

Graft

Tumor cells trajectory using slingshot

Audrey

2023-05-10

This script is used to infer trajectory on tumor cells, using slingshot.

library(dplyr)
library(patchwork)
library(ggplot2)

.libPaths()
## [1] "/usr/local/lib/R/library"

Preparation

In this section, we set the global settings of the analysis. We will store data there :

save_name = "graft_tumor_cells"
out_dir = "."

We load the sample information :

sample_info = readRDS(file = paste0(out_dir, "/../1_metadata/graft_sample_info.rds"))
project_names_oi = as.character(sample_info$project_name)

graphics::pie(rep(1, nrow(sample_info)),
              col = sample_info$color,
              labels = sample_info$project_name)

This are the settings for trajectory inference :

traj_dimred = "mnn"          # to infer trajectory on
traj_umap = "mnn_dm_umap"    # just of visualization
seed = 1337L
traj_max_dims = 46           # to infer trajectory on

We load the Seurat object :

sobj = readRDS(paste0(out_dir, "/", save_name, "_sobj.rds"))
sobj
## An object of class Seurat 
## 16731 features across 2835 samples within 2 assays 
## Active assay: RNA (14731 features, 2000 variable features)
##  1 other assay present: mnn.reconstructed
##  8 dimensional reductions calculated: RNA_pca, RNA_pca_46_tsne, RNA_pca_46_umap, mnn, mnn_46_tsne, mnn_46_umap, mnn_dm, mnn_dm_umap

Trajectory inference

We perform the trajectory inference :

input_data = sobj@reductions[[traj_dimred]]@cell.embeddings[, c(1:traj_max_dims)]
root_cell_id = "2020_18_AGGTCATTCTTTCCAA-1"

my_traj = slingshot::slingshot(data = input_data,
                               clusterLabels = sobj$seurat_clusters,
                               start.clus   = sobj$seurat_clusters[root_cell_id])
my_traj
## class: SlingshotDataSet 
## 
##  Samples Dimensions
##     2835         46
## 
## lineages: 2 
## Lineage1: 3  5  9  1  4  8  2  0  6  
## Lineage2: 3  5  9  1  7  
## 
## curves: 2 
## Curve1: Length: 1.5663   Samples: 2667.45
## Curve2: Length: 1.5256   Samples: 1239.96
## Add pseudotime
sobj$pseudotime = slingshot::slingPseudotime(my_traj)[, 1][colnames(sobj)]

(Time to run : 35.25 s)

There are two trajectories :

  • going from the root to the grafted tumor
  • going from the root to the cells expressing a high level of mitochondrial genes, from the donor (dead end)
head(slingshot::slingPseudotime(my_traj))
##                               curve1    curve2
## 2020_18_AAAGGGCAGCGCCATC-1 0.5926637 0.6006346
## 2020_18_AAAGTGAGTCGATTTG-1 0.3451827 0.3456193
## 2020_18_AAAGTGATCTCAGAAC-1 0.6152880 0.6119501
## 2020_18_AAATGGAAGCTCTGTA-1 0.4403344 0.4438892
## 2020_18_AACAACCTCGGTATGT-1 0.4915529 0.4942314
## 2020_18_AACAGGGTCATGCCCT-1 0.3283206 0.3293064

Visualization

We visualize pseudotime :

Seurat::FeaturePlot(sobj, features = "pseudotime",
                    reduction = traj_umap,
                    cols = viridis::viridis(n = 100)) +
  ggplot2::ggtitle("Pseudotime") +
  ggplot2::theme(aspect.ratio = 1,
                 plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) +
  Seurat::NoAxes()

Save

We save the Seurat object :

saveRDS(sobj, file = paste0(out_dir, "/", save_name, "_sobj_traj_slingshot.rds"))

We save the trajectory object :

saveRDS(my_traj, file = paste0(out_dir, "/", save_name, "_my_traj_slingshot.rds"))

R Session

show
